quiz.question:
You have the Black pieces.

Evaluate space and material. 

In terms of king safety, both sides have defended against a flank pawn attack. 

In terms of activity, who is better?

feedback.text:
This position is actually entirely balanced in terms of dynamics: White has a pawn on a6, Black has a pawn on h3. 

White controls the c-file, but Black has a pin on the f3 knight. 

White has a knight on d6. But Black has a knight on f5 and a bishop on f8 that can trade that knight off and so they can get rid of that active piece. 

This is a beautiful example of dynamic equality.
This evaluation implies that White has a break through in the attack, or that White will win material soon. Is that the case?
While Black has a strong pin on the f3 knight, White also has some merits to their position, so we cannot evaluate this as being nearly winning for Black.
While you could make an argument for this, you could also make an argument for Black's activity. Black will get rid of the knight on d6 soon, and they'll contest the c-file in no time.
